name: Capital One SavorOne Cash Rewards Credit Card

welcome_offer.description: Earn a one-time $200 cash bonus once you spend $500 on purchases within the first 3 months from account opening

earning_rates.notes: Excludes superstores like Walmart and Target. Includes supermarkets, meat lockers, freezer, dairy product stores and specialty markets.
Restaurants, cafes, bars, lounges, fast-food chains and bakeries

Movie theaters, sports events, theatrical promoters, amusement parks, tourist attractions, aquariums, zoos, dance halls, record stores, pool halls, bowling alleys. Excludes golf courses and streaming services.

other_benefits: Access to Capital One Shopping and Capital One Entertainment

sources.notes: Capital One official SavorOne page confirming 3% on dining, groceries, entertainment, streaming and $39 annual fee
Bankrate article on SavorOne $39 annual fee update (August 2025)
Capital One SavorOne overview confirming earning categories and $200 welcome bonus

notes: Capital One rebranded SavorOne with a $39 annual fee (previously $0). The $200 welcome bonus offer remains the same. Some Redditors and reviewers have noted the $39 annual fee makes the card less attractive versus the $0 Savor card or other 3% dining cards. Note: There is also a new Capital One Savor card ($0 annual fee) which may be a replacement or alternative to this product.
